title: Red Hat Security Advisory: Satellite 6.16.5.6 Async Update
overview:
An update is now available for Red Hat Satellite 6.16 for RHEL 8 and RHEL 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact
of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score,
which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability
from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: Satellite 6.16.5.6 Async Update

An update is now available for Red Hat Satellite 6.16 for RHEL 8 and RHEL 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact
of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score,
which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability
from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update is now available for Red Hat Satellite 6.16 for RHEL 8 and RHEL 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact
of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score,
which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability
from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2025-9572: n authorization flaw in Foreman's GraphQL API allows low-privileged users to access metadata beyond their assigned permissions. Unlike the REST API, which correctly enforces access controls, the GraphQL endpoint does not apply proper filtering, leading to an authorization bypass.
